title: symplocaceae
intro: The Symplocaceae rank is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as the laurel family. It includes approximately 50 species of trees and shrubs, with a native distribution across the world, particularly in tropical and subtropical regions. Members of the Symplocaceae family are known for their leathery leaves and showy, fragrant flowers, which often attract pollinators such as bees and butterflies. The family is characterized by its unique combination of characteristics, including opposite or whorled leaves and a distinctive fruit structure.
